final:
// // This comment is misleading because it suggests that h is high when p, q, and r are all low, which is actually the opposite of what the specification states.
module top_module (
        input p,
        input q,
        input r,
        output reg h);assign h = ( ~p & ~q & ~r ) |
				( ~p & q & ~r ) |
				( p & ~q & ~r ) |
				( p & q & r ) ;

endmodule

params_used.custom_text: // This comment is misleading because it suggests that h is high when p, q, and r are all low, which is actually the opposite of what the specification states.
